headline: Marine Le Pen to find out whether she can run for president
description: A court will rule on Tuesday in Marine Le Pen's appeal trial over alleged misappropriation of European Union funds. Its decision will determine whether she can run in the 2027 presidential election.

headline: The possible outcomes of Marine Le Pen's trial

US leads international concern after China test-fires missile into Pacific
description: Though a spokesperson for China's navy stated on Monday that the test launch was simply a 'routine arrangement' of military training, the US state department is concerned that this demonstrates China's growing capacity to strike the US mainland.

headline: 'A despicable woman unworthy of her office': Kylian Mbappé responds to racist remarks from Paraguayan senator
description: The captain of the French national football team responded on Monday to a racist message posted by Celeste Amarilla. The Paraguayan politician's comments came in the wake of the Albirroja's defeat by Les Bleus in the round of 16 of the World Cup.

headline: 2026 World Cup: Spain beat Portugal 1-0 to reach quarter-finals
description: The European champions will face the United States, the only one of the co-hosts remaining, or Belgium on Friday in Los Angeles.

headline: Somaliland's high-risk quest for recognition
description: The self-proclaimed republic aspires to accede to the international stage. It finds itself at the center of a game of alliances, caught between Israeli, American and Emirati ambitions and threats from Yemen and neighboring Somalia.

Resigned Le Pen puts on brave face before crucial court decision
description: In her final public appearance before the ruling on Tuesday that will determine whether she can run for president, Marine Le Pen focused on highlighting her partnership with Jordan Bardella.

headline: 'What comes next will be just as grueling': Villagers remain anxious after wildfire in southern France
description: A wildfire fanned by fierce winds amid extreme heat consumed 540 hectares of scrubland in the Gard department within a matter of hours.

headline: Vladimir Putin, or the loneliness of 'Homo Sovieticus'
description: Between denial of reality, a desire for revenge and carefully crafted messaging, the Russian president has tried to position himself as the master of the game. But after more than four years of war in Ukraine, these old tricks inherited from the Soviet past are showing their limitations, and he is losing credibility.
